title: The Alarm Never Waits
tagline: A structure fire, a possible occupant inside, and a commander who needs an answer in seconds.

wake_up.text: Twenty-four on, forty-eight off — today's the first of three. The station's quiet for now, coffee going, gear checked and re-checked out of habit more than need.

choices.text: Do a full, unhurried check of your gear before anything else
Quick check — you know it's fine

morning_drill.text: Morning drill: ladder throws and hose deployment, the same reps that make everything else automatic when it counts.

choices.text: Push the drill harder than usual
Run it at the standard pace

random_moment.text: The rest of the morning settles into the ordinary rhythm of station life — the small, different things that fill the space between calls.

choices.text: A rookie asks a good question you enjoy answering
A minor equipment hiccup during inspection, quickly fixed
Someone actually cooks a real breakfast for the crew

first_call.text: First tone of the day: a car fire, contained, no injuries. Routine, but nothing about pulling gear on ever feels fully routine.

choices.text: Do a thorough check of the surrounding area before clearing
Clear the scene quickly once the fire's out

structure_fire_dispatch.text: Tones drop again, different this time — structure fire, residential, smoke showing on the initial report. Your crew is first due.

choices.text: Roll out

on_scene_size_up.text: Heavy smoke from the second floor, no visible flame from the street yet. A neighbor is shouting that someone might still be inside.

choices.text: Report the size-up to command

search_decision.text: Command needs a call, now: the neighbor's report is unconfirmed, the smoke is thickening, and every second changes the odds inside.

choices.text: Go in for a primary search immediately
Wait the ninety seconds for the second engine before entering
Ventilate the roof first to improve conditions before entry
Call for a defensive attack from outside — structural risk is too high

search_outcome_immediate.text: Low and fast through the smoke, you find the bedroom and the person inside, disoriented but alive. Out the window and down the ladder in under two minutes.

choices.text: Get them to EMS and go back to work

search_outcome_wait.text: The second engine arrives and the search goes in as a two-team operation, exactly by protocol. It works, and it takes the extra ninety seconds it was always going to take.

choices.text: Continue the operation

search_outcome_ventilate.text: The roof cut clears the smoke layer fast, and the search that follows is faster and safer for it. A little more time up front bought a lot more visibility.

choices.text: Continue the operation

search_outcome_defensive.text: The call goes defensive. The structure is fully involved within minutes of your pullback — a call that, on the numbers, was almost certainly the right one, and still doesn't feel clean.

choices.text: Continue the operation

afternoon_lull.text: Back at the station, gear cleaned, air bottles refilled. The afternoon stretches out with nothing on the board — for now.

choices.text: Actually rest while you can
Stay geared up and alert instead of fully resting

medical_call.text: A medical call: elderly resident, difficulty breathing. Your crew arrives before the ambulance.

choices.text: Take charge of initial care until EMS arrives
Stabilize the basics and wait for EMS to take over

training_moment.text: Evening training block: today it's a new rookie's first real run-through of extraction techniques, and they're nervous about it.

choices.text: Take the extra time to mentor them through it properly
Give brief corrections and move the group along

night_call.text: A late call: a kitchen fire, small, out by the time you arrive, but the family is badly shaken.

choices.text: Take the extra few minutes to reassure the family properly
Wrap up quickly — it's late and you're due back at 6

end_shift.text: Back at the station, gear hung, ready for whatever the rest of the night brings — or doesn't.

choices.text: Try to get some sleep before the next tone

triumphant.title: The Save
triumphant.blurb: Every call held, and one of them will be talked about at the station for years. This is the shift that reminds everyone why they signed up.

steady_hand.title: Solid Shift
steady_hand.blurb: No headlines, no hesitation. Just careful, competent work on every call that came in.

ordinary_day.title: Another Twenty-Four
ordinary_day.blurb: A normal shift by the job's own strange standard — a structure fire, a medical call, and everyone went home the same way they came in.

burned_out.title: Running on Fumes
burned_out.blurb: You made it to shift change, but you're not sure how. This pace, call after call, has a real cost — rest before the next one.

written_up.title: After-Action Review
written_up.blurb: A few of today's calls are heading into a formal after-action review. Everyone's safe. The department still wants to understand the decisions.
